stem: A manufacturing quality inspection uses 3 parallel inspectors for redundancy. The defect caught requires at least one inspector to function. inspector 1 has a reliability of 91%. inspector 2 has a reliability between 73% and 91% (uncertain due to limited operational data). inspector 3 has a reliability of 75%. What is the overall probability that the defect caught?

ground_truth.answer: P(defect caught) ranges from 0.9939 to 0.998. Point estimate: 0.996.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 inspectors fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.09 × 0.27 × 0.25 = 0.0061. P(works) = 0.9939.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.09 × 0.09 × 0.25 = 0.002. P(works) = 0.998.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
